pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and setup_opentoonz.sh scripts use this exact path, sourced from official github.com/opentoonz/opentoonz_sample
setup_task.sh creates this directory and export_result.sh checks for output files here
Task description explicitly requires 1280x720 resolution and verifier.py enforces it
Task description explicitly requires 1280x720 resolution and verifier.py enforces it
Task description explicitly states 'render only frames 1 through 16'
Task description explicitly states 'render only frames 1 through 16'
Task description requests 'exactly this frame range' (1 through 16, totaling 16 frames)
verifier.py implements partial or full scoring logic based on a minimum acceptable threshold of 14
verifier.py implements partial or full scoring logic based on a maximum acceptable threshold of 20
verifier.py directly enforces a 100 KB size minimum on the generated output

privileged_info_summary: Completed PI audit for opentoonz_env web_delivery_trim task, verifying that real sample data (dwanko_run.tnz) from the official OpenToonz GitHub repository is used. The original scene contains 72 frames. The expected output directory, target resolution of 1280x720, and output size threshold of 100 KB exactly match the task parameters and verifier scripts. The request of trimming the animation exactly to frames 1-16 matches the instructions, with the verifier correctly scoring frame counts in the 14-20 range.
